plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad market exposure.
Buy XLF at 5% to diversify into a high-scoring financial sector.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a modest ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely due to being below stop and reducing leverage.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to manage single name concentration.
Buy PANW at 5% to add a high-scoring software position.
Buy MSFT at 5% to diversify into another quality tech name.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month growth with a risk-first approach. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality tech, but market volatility remains a factor.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL completely as it's below stop and reallocate capital.
Trim SPAL to 2% to maintain a small leveraged ETF position.
Add to CRWD, increasing to 18%, to press a strong performing AI/Software name.
Buy PANW at 10% to add a high-scoring software setup.
Buy NVDA at 10% to add a high-scoring semiconductor setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month growth by leaning into high-scoring setups and maintaining some leverage. This mode carries significantly higher risk, including potential for larger drawdowns and daily decay from leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for income.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Trim SMH to 5% to reduce high-beta sector exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize income-focused exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month distributions by rotating into income-focused ETFs and broad index. While aiming for low drawdown and reliable income, distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
